response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but misses nearly every hard requirement for this role. There is no indication of Japanese proficiency (a stated native-level requirement), no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience, no L2/L3 networking or network-switch background, and no exposure to the listed security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, captive portal). His C experience is brief and dated (a seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 plus a C++ thesis project); his career centers on web and full-stack work in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. This is a fundamental profile mismatch rather than a skills gap that interviewing could clarify.
strengths: Deep, broad engineering experience (15+ years) spanning web, mobile, desktop, and low-latency backend systems
Some systems-level background: C/C++ components at National Instruments, Linux-based audio thesis, high-performance DLang services
Strong software-craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, teaching and workshops)
concerns: No Japanese language ability listed; native-level Japanese is a hard requirement
No experience with Linux kernel modification, L2/L3 networking, network switches, or the required security technologies
Strong C proficiency for UNIX is required, but his C experience is minimal, brief, and roughly a decade old

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking, security, or Linux kernel experience
Solid general senior engineering track record
